title: galium palustre subsp. elongatum
intro: Galium palustre subsp. elongatum, also known as tall marsh bedstraw, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is a perennial herb with a history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Galium palustre subsp. elongatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, aid in wound healing, and act as a diuretic, making it useful in treating urinary tract issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used topically in the form of creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
